questions: Is a 45 year old male covered for knee replacement surgery done 2 months after policy inception?

answers.answer: The 45-year-old male is not covered for knee replacement surgery done 2 months after policy inception because it falls within the 24-month waiting period for specified diseases/procedures.
answers.justification: Specified disease/procedure waiting period (Code-Excl02) excludes expenses related to the treatment of listed conditions, surgeries/treatments until the expiry of 24 months of continuous coverage.

relevant_clauses.section: SECTION D) EXCLUSIONS- STANDARD EXCLUSIONS APPLICABLE TO PART A- DOMESTIC COVER
relevant_clauses.content: Expenses related to the treatment of the listed Conditions, surgeries/treatments shall be excluded until the expiry of 24 months of continuous coverage after the date of inception of the first Global Health Care Policy with Us.
